sections.paragraph:
an unused root. Arab. كَادَ Med. Ye, to use deceit, prop., I believe, to ensnare, so that it is cognate to the verbs אָכַד, אָגַד, and others, with which it is compared under that root. Hence كَيْدُ deceit, snares; also destruction, ruin, war. So the Hebr.-

כיד

m. destruction, calamity, Job 21:20. See also כִּידֹון.
